content.problemStatement: Identify and rectify the errors in the code provided to get the desired output.
content.inputFormat: If the input is empty or missing, assume T = 1. Otherwise the first line contains a single integer T denoting the number of repetitions. No further input is required.
content.outputFormat: Print the three lines in the exact order: "Coding Chef", "Chafe", "Coding Chafe". Repeat this block T times, each line separated by a newline character. The output ends with a newline after the last line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: No input is provided, so the program defaults to T = 1 and prints the three lines once.

sampleTestCases.output:
Coding Chef
Chafe
Coding Chafe
Coding Chef
Chafe
Coding Chafe
sampleTestCases.explanation: T = 2, therefore the block of three lines is printed twice.
T = 1, the block is printed exactly once.

solution.approach: Read the optional integer T from standard input. If reading fails (no input or non‑numeric), default T to 1. Then iterate i from 0 to T‑1 and output the three required strings, each followed by a newline. The algorithm is O(T) time and O(1) auxiliary space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – one constant‑time output per line, three lines per repetition.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few scalar variables are used, output is streamed directly to stdout.
